title: Ayyayo En Usurukulla Song Lyrics Paruthiveeran
stanzas:
yelae yelaeley
yelae yelaeley
oththa pana oraththula
sethaneram un madiyil
thala vachi saanjikiren
sangathiya sollithaaren
vaadi nee vaadi

pathu kannu paalathula
maechalukku kaathiruppen
paaichaloda vaadi
koocham keecham thevaiyilla
vaadi nee vaadi
yelae yelaeley
yelae yelaeley

sevvelani chinna kani
unna sirai edukka poren
vaa nee

ayyaiyyo en usurukkulla
theeya vachaan ayyaiyyo
ayyaiyyo en manasukulla
noiya thachaan ayyaiyyo

sandaali un paasathaale
naanum sundeliyaa aanen pulla
nee konnaa kooda kuthamilla
nee sonnaa saagum indha pulla

ayyaiyyo
en vetkam paththi
vegurathe ayyaiyyo
en samanja dhegam
saaiyirathe ayyaiyyo

arali vedha paasak kaari
aala kollum paasak kaari
en udambu nenje kili
nee ulla vandha getti kaari

ayyaiyyo
en iduppu vetti
irangi poche ayyaiyyo
en meesa murukkum
madangi poche ayyaiyyo

kallukkulla therai pola
kalanjirukkum thaadi kulla
olinjikka vaa

kaal suththum nizhala pola
potta kaatil unkoodave
thangidavaa

ayyanaara paathaale
un nenappu thaandaa
ammi kallu poo pola
maari pochu yenda

nee konnaa kooda kuthamilla
nee sonnaa saagum indhapulla

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses deep emotions of love and longing, reflecting the struggles and desires of the characters in the movie.

What is the plot context of the song in the movie?

In Paruthiveeran, the song plays during a pivotal moment that highlights the protagonist's feelings for his love interest.

Who is the singer and music director of this song?

The song is sung by Shreya Goshal and composed by Yuvan Shankar Raja.

When was the song released?

The song was released on February 23, 2007.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Ayyayo En Usurukulla, the listener is taken on an emotional journey through the eyes of the protagonist in Paruthiveeran. The song beautifully encapsulates the feelings of love and yearning, making it a poignant moment in the film. Shreya Goshal's voice adds a layer of depth, evoking nostalgia and melancholy. This melody resonates with anyone who has experienced the complexities of love.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Ayyayo En Usurukulla from Paruthiveeran is a hauntingly beautiful melody that captures the essence of love and longing. Shreya Goshal's soulful voice, combined with Yuvan's enchanting composition, creates an emotional experience that resonates deeply. This song is a must-listen for anyone who appreciates the depth of Tamil music and its storytelling.
stanzaMeanings: I am singing with my head held high in your lap, I am telling you the truth, you come, you come.
I am waiting for the milk in my ten eyes, there is no need for a fuss, you come, you come.
I will take you away, little fruit, come to me.
Oh my life, you have set fire to my heart, oh my heart, there is pain in my mind, oh my.
With your love, I have become beautiful, even if you are a little rough, this boy will not be a problem if you say so.
Oh my shame, my embarrassment has gone away, oh my mustache has also disappeared, oh my.
Like a stone in a well, I am stuck in this situation, come and help me.
Like a shadow that follows, will you stay with me?
Oh my heart, my stomach has burst, oh my mustache has also twisted, oh my.
Like a lamp in a stone, I am stuck in this situation, come and help me.
When I see you, my heart is filled with joy, like a flower that blooms in the garden.
I will not go, you stay here.
You are like a sharp knife, cutting through my heart.
With your love, I have become beautiful, even if you are a little rough, this boy will not be a problem if you say so.
